project.name: Bump
project.summary: Release automation CLI and GitHub Action that turns conventional commits and optional bump files into release PRs, changelogs, versions, and npm publication.
goals: Own the Bump CLI, GitHub Action contract, release PR generation, changelog generation, semantic version calculation, package detection, workspace handling, and npm publish orchestration.
Maintain tests, examples, action metadata, release workflow dogfooding, and documentation for release automation behavior.
Provide reusable release mechanics without encoding project-specific product, pricing, or roadmap policy.
nonGoals: Own every package repository's release readiness, package-specific API policy, pricing, roadmap, or changelog content.
Own GitHub, npm, runner fleet, organization secrets, package access control, or branch protection policy.
Own application delivery, deployment, database migration, or runtime rollback behavior.

owns.name: bump-cli
owns.description: The local CLI commands for status, bump files, release PR creation, version calculation, changelog generation, and publish orchestration.

owns.name: bump-github-action
owns.description: The composite GitHub Action interface in action.yml and the action behavior used by consuming repositories.

owns.name: release-detection-core
owns.description: Conventional commit parsing, workspace/package detection, cascade bump logic, changelog rendering, and npm publish mechanics.

doesNotOwn: Package-specific release intent, API compatibility decisions, pricing, roadmap, or product launch decisions for consuming repositories.
GitHub branch protection, npm org permissions, runner fleet capacity, or central CI admission policy.
Runtime deployment, canary, rollback, or database recovery flows.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema, runner policy, CI admission doctrine, and agent execution standards

forbiddenCouplings: Do not hardcode a consuming repository's product-specific release, pricing, roadmap, or package policy into Bump.
Do not make Bump require Sylphx-only deployment, runtime, or infrastructure assumptions for normal public package use.
Do not treat Bump as the source of truth for package readiness; consuming repositories must own their tests, release intent, and publication proof.

delivery.deployPath: Pull requests and merge groups run the repository CI workflow. Main release automation dogfoods the local action through .github/workflows/release.yml.
delivery.productionProof: Package-impacting work must prove CI, tests, typecheck, build output, action contract compatibility, release PR behavior where affected, and npm version readback after publication.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Conventional commits and optional bump files are Bump's Changesets-equivalent release intent; the repo-local adoption gap tracks the ADR for when Bump is approved instead of Changesets.
packageRelease.versionPr: Bump creates or updates a bot/GitHub App-owned release PR, then publishes after the release PR merge when running in auto mode; the current workflow dogfoods the local action while the release-bot path is certified.
packageRelease.publisher: The protected GitHub Actions release workflow publishes with NPM_TOKEN as a bounded token fallback until npm OIDC/trusted publishing and provenance are adopted.
packageRelease.publishProof: Release proof is the Release workflow conclusion, npm registry readback for @sylphx/bump, GitHub action tag/readme verification when relevant, and provenance or attestation evidence when trusted publishing is adopted.

commercial.decisionHome: README.md and future ADRs for material packaging or positioning changes.
commercial.pricingSourceOfTruth: No product pricing source of truth; Bump is an open-source release automation package and GitHub Action.
commercial.metricsSourceOfTruth: npm package downloads, GitHub Action usage, issue reports, CI results, and release workflow telemetry.
guardrails: Do not add pricing, packaging, or commercial positioning changes without ADR-backed market and usage evidence.
Release behavior changes must preserve consuming repository ownership of release intent and publish proof.

gaps.description: Bump uses repo-local legacy CI contexts rather than central ADR-29 admission contexts.
gaps.target: Adopt central admission workflow when the shared package/tool template is available.

gaps.description: Bump intentionally dogfoods Bump instead of Changesets; central doctrine should document when Bump versus Changesets is the approved package release path.
gaps.target: Package release doctrine ADR and shared workflow catalog.
